question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $100,000 on November 18?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Heatmap Analysis Reveals BTC's Critical Price Levels
evidences.content: View analytics → Prediction Banner Bitcoin’s price hovers at $95,401, with recent activity concentrated between $95,000 and $100,000. The Binance BTC/USDT heatmap reveals high-leverage zones, highlighting potential short squeezes and long liquidation risks. Bright clusters in the $95,000 to $100,000 range suggest high-volume liquidation sweeps due to sharp market moves. Bitcoin’s price is around the $95,000 zone as of January 9, 2025, with critical liquidity clusters forming between $94,000 and $100,000. The Binance BTC/USDT liquidation heatmap shows several high-leverage zones where traders are at risk, offering a snapshot of the market’s current liquidity dynamics. [...] The heatmap shows the price ranges to be between $80,000 and $120,000, but most of the action will be in the $95,000 to $100,000 range. Analysts point to these zones as pivotal for Bitcoin’s next big move. BTC/USD Liquidation HeatMap that predicts the price levels at which large-scale BTC liquidation to occur. BTC/USDT Liquidation heatmap (Source: Coinglass) One key zone of interest lies above $94,000, where a bunch of short positions could trigger a short squeeze if Bitcoin’s price pushes higher. evidences.content: ``` 1688169600000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,0.54114000,1688169600999,16489.61790060,31,0.52320000,15942.95040000,0 ``` enables you to calculate this: From millisecond timestamp `1688169600000` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00 UTC) To millisecond timestamp `1688169600999` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00.999 UTC) Full candle + Open price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC + Close price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC + Close is higher than open => candle is green Knots + Lowest price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as open price of green candle => no knot below + Highest price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as close price of green candle => no knot above Share Improve this answer answered Aug 21, 2023 at 21:45 Petr Hejda's user avatar Petr Hejda [...] ``` #!/bin/bash for i in $(seq -f "%02g" 1 31) # zero-padded days 01, 02, 03, ... 31 do # month of july 2023, day number is the `i` variable archiveName="BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-${i}.zip" # download the .zip, extract it (saves .csv), and remove the .zip wget " && unzip "./${archiveName}" && rm "./${archiveName}" done ``` As mentioned in their GitHub readme, each of the the `.csv` lines contains the following field values: | Open time...

evidences.title: BTC/USDT 1-Minute Historical CSV & Excel Download
evidences.content: ### Instructions 1. Select an exchange and choose one or more cryptocurrency pairs (for example BTCUSD, ETHUSD or XRPUSD). By moving the mouse over each pair you can see how much historical data is available for download. 2. Select at least one candlestick timeframe such as 1 minute, 5 minutes, hourly or daily bars. CryptoDatum provides historical OHLCV data from 1m up to 1d. 3. Choose your preferred format (CSV, Excel or ForexTester). Format samples are shown in the section below. 4. Pay only for the datasets you selected (5.99 € per dataset – VAT included). 5. Wait while we prepare a ZIP archive containing your historical crypto datasets. 6. Download your datasets using the provided link (it will also be sent to your email). ### What data is included in the datasets? [...] #### Standard CSV (Python) `time, open, high, low, close, volume 1364774400, 100.0, 93.03, 93.25, 93.03, 247.28` ### Frequently Asked Questions1. What exchanges are supported? CryptoDatum currently provides historical cryptocurrency data from Binance and Bitfinex. #### 2. What timeframes are available? Datasets can be downloaded with candlestick intervals from 1 minute (1m) to 1 day (1d). #### 3. What fields...

evidences.title: candlestick start and end dates - what is the time zone? · Issue #51 · sonvister/Binance · GitHub
evidences.content: ## Navigation Menu # Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests... # Provide feedback We read every piece of feedback, and take your input very seriously. # Saved searches ## Use saved searches to filter your results more quickly To see all available qualifiers, see our documentation. # candlestick start and end dates - what is the time zone? #51 pavlexander ## Description @pavlexander Short question - what is the datetime zone for candlesticks? I was thinking that it's in UTC, however, after calling the API a couple of times - I am feeling lost. At the moment of writing current UTC time is: 2/8/2018 12:06:11 PM Latest candle close dateime from API is: 2/8/2018 12:28:14 AM Which is 12 hours difference. [...] This does not make any sense. It feels like that new candles are either added by binance with some delay, or candle timezone is -12 (average?? not exact) hours from UTC. Therefore following clarifications are necessary: P.s. in my tests I was using 5 minute interval. Thanks in advance, Aleksandrs ## Metadata ## Metadata ### Assignees @pavlexander ### Labels ### Projects ### Milestone ### Relationships ### Development ## Issue actions ## Footer ### Footer navigation

evidences.title: Bitcoin Retreats to $100,000—What’s Next for the Crypto Market? | Morningstar
evidences.content: Kimmell says, “a genuine crypto winter typically has ongoing waves of leverage liquidations, strong exchange outflows, large wallet supply activations, declining institutional interest, crypto industry bankruptcies, and a risk-off macro backdrop.” Instead, he expects bitcoin to remain “range-bound with periods of volatility tied to macro data, regulation, and market positioning,” while staying optimistic about the medium-term outlook as monetary conditions ease and institutional participation deepens. Ndinga frames short-term volatility as “healthy” after a long rally and record inflows, maintaining that “as long as BTC holds above $100,000, the structural uptrend remains intact.” [...] Morningstar ## Table of Contents # Bitcoin Retreats to $100,000—What’s Next for the Crypto Market? Cryptocurrency’s slide reignited investor jitters, but analysts say institutional demand and macro forces should provide support. ollage illustration featuring Bitcoins, a ticker board, and an investor looking at their phone. ### Key Takeaways Bitcoin’s 13% pullback over the past month has rattled a market that spent most of the last two years celebrating new cryptocurrency highs, record ETF inflows...

evidences.title: Crypto Trading Hours: Best Time to Trade Crypto
evidences.content: ### What is the best time to trade Bitcoin (BTC)? + The best time to trade Bitcoin is during the US-Europe overlap, between 13:00 and 17:00 UTC. This window produces the tightest spreads, deepest order books, and most consistent price discovery for BTC across both spot and derivatives markets. Bitcoin's liquidity is more concentrated around US trading hours than any other cryptocurrency: institutional investors account for approximately 63% of BTC trading volume during US business hours, and the launch of spot Bitcoin ETFs has reinforced this concentration. CPI releases at 13:30 UTC and FOMC rate decisions at 19:00 UTC produce the largest single-event BTC price moves. Traders looking to trade Bitcoin through CFDs should prioritise this window for the lowest execution costs. [...] | Time window (UTC) | Regional activity | Liquidity level | --- | 00:00–08:00 | Asia (Tokyo, Hong Kong, Seoul, Sydney) | Low to moderate | | 08:00–13:00 | Europe (London, Frankfurt, Zurich) | Moderate to high | | 13:00–17:00 | US–Europe overlap (London + New York) | Highest | | 17:00–22:00 | US (New York, post-overlap) | Moderate | | 22:00–00:00 | Transition (late US to early Asia) | Low | The lowest gl...

evidences.title: BTC/USDT Spot CVD Chart Analysis Reveals Critical Support Zones and Institutional Order Flow Patterns
evidences.content: Share: April 16, 2025 (05:00 UTC) BTC/USDT Spot CVD and Volume Heatmap across major CEXes (Binance, Coinbase, Kraken) identified bright volume zones that map key support/resistance levels for BTC. Multi-tier CVD by order size ($100–$1k retail, $1k–$10k mid, $10k–$100k wealthy, $100k–$1M institutional, $1M–$10M whale) showed institutional lines (red/brown: $100k–$10M) accumulating while retail flows weakened, a divergence that historically precedes broader rallies. Practical crypto trading implications: use CVD + heatmap confluence with TA (Fibonacci, MAs) for position sizing and risk management; signals point to increased institutional demand and potential short-to-medium-term bullish momentum for BTC. Predictions Markets See what traders are focused on Prediction Banner [...] ## Market Context and Historical Precedents The April 16 analysis occurs within a broader market context that experienced significant volatility throughout early 2025. Bitcoin’s price action during this period reflected ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty combined with evolving regulatory developments. Previous CVD analyses from March 2025 successfully predicted several key support levels that held during su...

evidences.title: $BTC Understanding the BTC/USDT Coin Pair on ...
evidences.content: BTC, as the first and most valuable cryptocurrency, is known for its volatility. Pairing it with USDT helps traders measure Bitcoin’s value in dollar terms and manage risk during market fluctuations. Since USDT maintains a 1:1 peg with the US dollar, it acts as a digital equivalent of cash, allowing traders to lock in profits or avoid losses during market dips without leaving the crypto ecosystem. On Binance, BTC/USDT is among the most liquid pairs, offering tight spreads, deep order books, and fast execution. This makes it ideal for both spot trading and derivatives. The pair is also frequently used in technical analysis and trading bots due to its steady volume and predictable behavior compared to altcoin pairs. [...] Overall, the BTC/USDT pair is a cornerstone of crypto trading. It combines the innovation of Bitcoin with the stability of a digital dollar, making it essential for portfolio management, market analysis, and day-to-day trading strategies on Binance. Disclaimer: Includes third-party opinions. No advice. Binance AI may be used without guarantee.See T&Cs. It comes as Strategy's unrealized losses have reached a record $10.8 billion. Market divided over what's next for Bitcoin. Bitcoin's price briefly dropped below $61,500 this week, pushing Strategy deeper into record unrealized losses and leaving more than half of all Bitcoin in circulation underwater. However, despite the mass selloff, Standard Chartered's Geoffrey Kendrick said he still expects the crypto to reach $100,000 by the end of the year. evidences.title: The Rhythm of Liquidity: Temporal Patterns in Market Depth
evidences.content: ## The Tale of Two Markets At 11:00 UTC on any given day, the Bitcoin orderbook on Binance shows $3.86 million in liquidity within 10 basis points of the mid-price. Market makers are active, spreads are tight, and a million-dollar order can execute with minimal impact. Fast forward ten hours to 21:00 UTC, and it's a different market entirely—the same BTC/FDUSD pair now shows only $2.71 million in depth, a 42% reduction. Same asset, same exchange, same day, but fundamentally different trading conditions. [...] ### Practical Takeaways The data delivers clear lessons: Best liquidity: Target 09:00-13:00 UTC for large orders Worst liquidity: Avoid 20:00-23:00 UTC unless necessary Expect 42% variation: Plan for significantly different conditions between peak and trough The ratio persists: The 1.42x peak/trough relationship appears consistently enough to build into execution algorithms The 24-hour liquidity cycle isn't merely a curiosity but a fundamental market structure feature. Those who respect its rhythm trade with the market's natural flow; those who ignore it pay the price in wider spreads and greater slippage. In crypto's always-on markets, knowing when to trade has become as c...

evidences.title: The Best Times to Trade Cryptocurrency: A Comprehensive Guide
evidences.content: ### Optimal Trading Hours Certain periods in a 24-hour cycle are more favorable for crypto trading due to increased market activity and liquidity. These periods often align with overlaps between major financial markets: #### Asian Session (12:00 AM - 7:00 AM UTC): This session marks the opening of major Asian exchanges like Binance and Huobi. It’s characterized by moderate volatility and is ideal for traders focusing on cryptocurrencies popular in Asia. #### European Session (8:00 AM - 4:00 PM UTC): As European markets open, trading volumes increase significantly. This session is particularly active during its overlap with the Asian markets in the morning and the American markets in the afternoon. #### American Session (1:00 PM - 9:00 PM UTC): [...] The U.S. session is often the most volatile due to its overlap with both European and Asian markets at different times. This period sees high liquidity and frequent price movements. For U.S.-based traders, early mornings (8:00 AM - 12:00 PM EST) are particularly advantageous as they coincide with European activity. Similarly, late afternoons (2:30 PM - 4:30 PM UTC) during the overlap of London and New York markets are prime trading h...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price History (2009-2026) - SoFi
evidences.content: ### 2023: $16,500 to $44,000 January 2023 saw Bitcoin’s price increase to around $23,300, sparking hopes that the crypto winter had begun to thaw. Meanwhile, other cryptocurrencies began showing similar price patterns in Q1. The rest of 2023 proved to be fruitful for those who were able to hold on through the crypto winter. At mid-year, Bitcoin’s price had topped $30,000 once again, and while there were some slight declines, the crypto finished the year strong. By December 2023, Bitcoin’s price notched a high of $44,705, before closing the year just above $42,000. ### 2024: $42,000 to $100,000+ [...] While 2014 opened at about $760, the price overall held steady in the $200 to $500 range for much of this time, briefly dipping below $200 in January and August of 2015. Bitcoin closed out 2015 at $430, marking a period of overall price stability. The official B symbol that has come to be associated with Bitcoin was adopted in November of that year. ### 2016: $430 to $960 In 2016, Bitcoin halved for a second time, prompting a notable jump in prices by year’s end. January ended the month with a closing price of $368, but by December, Bitcoin’s price had almost reached $1,000. A sligh...
